lead.title: Sunday, 17 May 2026
lead.standfirst: Bank of America Global Research has revised its Fed rate-cut forecast to July 2027, citing energy-driven inflation from the ongoing Iran conflict that has pushed headline PCE to 3.5% and core PCE to 3.

further_reading.title: Friday, 15 May 2026
further_reading.standfirst: President Trump wrapped up a two-day summit with President Xi Jinping in Beijing, covering the Iran war, trade, AI governance, and Taiwan. Xi told Trump that Taiwan is 'the most important issue in Chi.

further_reading.title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit: 200 Jets, Rare Earths Preserved, Truce Extended
further_reading.standfirst: Trump's two-day Beijing summit delivered a narrow set of trade commitments — 200 Boeing jets, beef imports restored, rare-earth flows preserved — while Taiwan, Iran, and AI chip export controls were all left unresolved, with Xi framing the outcome as 'constructive strategic stability' ahead of an October tariff deadline.

further_reading.title: Thursday, 14 May 2026
further_reading.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping held a two-day summit in Beijing — attended by top American CEOs including Elon Musk, Tim Cook, and Larry Fink — covering trade, the Iran war, and Taiw.

further_reading.title: Cloudflare Cuts 1,100 Jobs at Record Revenue
further_reading.standfirst: Cloudflare announced its first mass layoff in sixteen years on May 7 — 1,100 jobs eliminated alongside record quarterly revenue — and explicitly attributed the cut to internal AI agent adoption. The move is the clearest disclosed case yet of AI displacement at a growing company.

further_reading.title: Wall Street's AI Chip Rotation
further_reading.standfirst: AMD, Intel, and Micron have outrun Nvidia by orders of magnitude year-to-date as Wall Street rotates AI-hardware capital from training GPUs to inference-era CPUs and memory. Nvidia's counter-move is a 0 billion equity-investment campaign across its own customer base.

further_reading.title: Warsh Clears Cloture as Partisan Fed Confirmation Concludes
further_reading.standfirst: The Senate's Monday cloture vote on Kevin Warsh's Fed nomination — 49-44 on party lines — caps the most strictly partisan confirmation process in Fed Chair history. Warsh inherits a Fed pinned between an Iran-war energy shock and a shutdown-distorted core inflation print due Tuesday.
